title: Production-finish a modern vibrant abstract logo: clean, color-tune, vectorize, and ship full-colour + single-colour + print variants with a style note
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: the client wants a logo that "feels unmistakably modern... bright, energetic colours paired with clean, abstract shapes — no literal illustrations or vintage flourishes," that "look[s] just as sharp on a website header as it does on packaging or a social profile photo," and an explicit final hand-off of "full-colour and single-colour versions, vector source files (AI or SVG), and high-resolution PNG/JPEG exports, along with a simple style note covering hex/RGB values and any typography used." All of those deliverables and constraints are kept exactly. REASONABLY ENRICHED / RESHAPED to fit the Adobe connector honestly: the brief's literal opening step ("begin with a few quick concept sketches... we'll choose the strongest direction together") is human ideation and from-scratch abstract-mark drawing, which is in the connector's NOT-AVAILABLE list (no text-to-image, no from-scratch character/illustration art, no bespoke type drawing). So this benchmark begins AFTER concept selection: the client supplies the agreed raster rendering of the chosen abstract mark (their approved concept), and the agent performs the genuine PRODUCTION-AND-FINISHING work a logo freelancer does in Illustrator/Photoshop to turn that approved concept into the contracted hand-off package. The "couple of focused revision rounds" map to the connector's iterative tonal/colour/HSL passes. Nothing in the deliverable list is invented; the chain produces exactly the full-colour vector, single-colour version, print variant, and high-res raster exports the client asked for. Note: the candidate's original category field was "headshot_portrait" which is mismatched with the actual logo brief; the brief text governs and the category field is set to "vectorize_logo" to match the actual vectorize-and-ship logo production job.
one_line_ask: Take my approved raster abstract logo concept and produce the full hand-off package — cleaned and brightened full-colour vector master (AI/SVG), a single-colour version, a single-colour print/screen variant, a wide social-header lockup, high-res PNG/JPEG exports, and a style note with hex/RGB values and recommended typography.
full_brief: I'm refreshing my brand identity and we've already landed on the strongest concept direction together: a bright, energetic, clean abstract mark — modern, no literal illustrations, no vintage flourishes. I'm handing you the approved raster rendering of that mark (PNG with some edge fringing and a slightly off-square crop from the concept render) plus a small swatch/reference image of the exact brand colours I want to lock to. Please turn this into the final production package.\n\nConcretely: (1) straighten and tightly crop the mark so it sits square and centered; (2) normalize the tone, then push the colours so they read as bright and energetic — lift vibrance/saturation and tune the individual hues until they hit the brand harmony, not muddy; (3) knock out the background to clean transparency so the mark drops onto any header, packaging, or profile photo; (4) from that clean mark, produce the FULL-COLOUR VECTOR MASTER (AI/SVG source) so it stays razor-sharp at any size; (5) produce the SINGLE-COLOUR version (one flat brand colour on transparent) for stamps, embossing and one-ink contexts, by selecting the mark and laying a single flat brand ink over it; (6) produce a single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant (halftone/duotone) for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing; (7) produce a wide website/social-header crop by reframing the clean full-colour master to a wide banner aspect (cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark) so it can sit as a header strip; (8) export everything as high-resolution PNG and JPEG; and (9) give me a simple style note: the exact hex/RGB values pulled from the locked palette plus a recommended typography pairing that suits this modern, energetic personality.

inputs.name: approved_abstract_mark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A single modern abstract brand logo mark centered on a plain pale-grey studio background, rendered as if exported from a concept design tool. The mark is a clean geometric abstract form — three overlapping rounded chevron/petal shapes fanning out, no letters, no literal objects, no realistic illustration. Bright energetic gradient palette: electric magenta into coral-orange into a vivid cyan accent. Slightly off-square framing with a few pixels of empty margin uneven on each side, a faint anti-aliased fringe / light halo at the shape edges against the grey, and a barely-perceptible 1.5-degree rotation so it is not perfectly level. Flat vector-style shading, crisp but with the subtle softness of a raster export, 2048x1900 px. No text, no wordmark, no drop shadow, no mockup, no hand, no background scene.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real approved concept hand-off render: a raster PNG (not already a clean vector), with genuine production defects the agent has to fix — slight rotation (drives auto_straighten), uneven margin (drives crop_to_bounds), edge fringe/halo against grey (drives remove_background), and slightly flat/dull colour before the vibrance+HSL pass. Abstract geometric only, so it honours the brief's 'no literal illustrations' and is legitimately vectorizable.

inputs.name: brand_palette_swatch.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A simple horizontal brand colour swatch strip on white, five flat solid rectangles edge to edge with no gaps: electric magenta (#E6007E), coral-orange (#FF5A36), vivid cyan (#00C2D1), near-black ink (#16161D), and a soft off-white (#F4F2EC). Thin hairline separators, tiny tick marks, no text labels, no hex numbers printed, clean studio-flat lighting, 1600x400 px. This is a colour reference card, not a design.
inputs.realism_notes: A real client-supplied palette reference. Used as the source of truth for the 'hex/RGB values' style-note deliverable and to ground the HSL / single-colour fill choices. No printed text so it reads as a swatch card, not an infographic.

outputs.name: logo_fullcolour_master.svg
outputs.spec: Full-colour vector master of the cleaned, brightened, background-removed abstract mark. Produced by image_vectorize from the finished raster. Razor-sharp at any size; this is the AI/SVG source file the brief contracts for.

outputs.name: logo_fullcolour_master.png
outputs.spec: High-resolution transparent PNG of the full-colour mark, exported from the cleaned raster (post background-removal). >=2000 px on the long edge, alpha transparency, brand-accurate brightened colours.

outputs.name: logo_fullcolour_master.jpg
outputs.spec: High-resolution JPEG of the full-colour mark, exported from the same cleaned master raster. >=2000 px on the long edge, sRGB, flattened onto the brand off-white (#F4F2EC) since JPEG has no alpha. This is the JPEG half of the contracted high-res PNG/JPEG raster exports.

outputs.name: logo_singlecolour.png
outputs.spec: Single-colour (one flat brand ink, magenta #E6007E) version of the mark on transparency, produced by selecting the mark subject and laying a flat colour overlay over the selection (image_select_subject -> image_apply_color_overlay). For one-ink stamping, embossing, foil and dark/light backgrounds.

outputs.name: logo_singlecolour_halftone_print.png
outputs.spec: Single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant: halftone/duotone treatment of the mark for low-ink packaging and merch screen-printing. Crisp dot pattern, one ink.

outputs.name: logo_social_header_crop.png
outputs.spec: Wide website/social-header crop: the clean full-colour master REFRAMED to a wide banner aspect (approx 3:1) by cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark via image_crop_and_resize (gen-free crop; no canvas expansion, no compositing, output not larger than the source in the widened dimension). Usable as a header strip; PNG with alpha transparency.

outputs.name: logo_social_header_crop.jpg
outputs.spec: JPEG copy of the wide website/social-header crop, exported from the same reframed wide slice. sRGB, flattened onto the brand off-white (#F4F2EC) since JPEG has no alpha. This is the JPEG half of the header-strip high-res PNG/JPEG exports.

outputs.name: brand_style_note.txt
outputs.spec: Simple style note: exact hex/RGB values for the locked palette (#E6007E, #FF5A36, #00C2D1, #16161D, #F4F2EC) plus a recommended typography pairing (from font_recommend) suited to a modern energetic brand.

connector_workflow.note: Begin upload of the client's approved raster mark to CC storage; returns the upload target/presigned slot.
Finalize the upload into a working CC asset (the raster the whole chain edits).
Correct the ~1.5deg tilt from the concept render so the mark sits perfectly level.
Trim the uneven margin to a tight, centered, square bound around the mark.
Baseline tonal normalization before the colour push so the brightening is even.
Lift vibrance/saturation to deliver the brief's 'bright, energetic colours'.
Tune individual hues toward the locked palette harmony (magenta/coral/cyan) so colours read clean, not muddy. Palette swatch is the colour reference.
Knock out the grey background + edge fringe to clean transparency. This is the master full-colour raster (also the high-res PNG export).
Convert the clean full-colour mark to a sharp SVG/AI vector master — the contracted vector source file.
Select the mark shapes so a single flat ink can be laid over exactly the subject for the single-colour version.

Lay one flat brand ink (magenta #E6007E) over the selected mark subject on transparency -> single-colour version. Flat recolour of the selected subject, no generation (replaces the prior invert->fill_area transparent-PNG-as-mask anti-pattern).
Apply a halftone/duotone screen to the single-colour mark -> low-ink packaging / merch screen-print variant.

Reframe the clean full-colour master to a wide website/social-header aspect (approx 3:1) by cropping to a wide horizontal slice of the mark. Gen-free crop only — no canvas expansion, no compositing; output not larger than the source in the widened dimension.
Recommend a typography pairing matching the modern, energetic personality for the style note.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-2 load the client raster into a working CC asset (mark_raster_asset). Each finishing pass consumes the previous step's output: straighten(3)->crop(4)->auto_tone(5)->vibrance(6)->HSL brand-tune(7, also reading the palette swatch)->remove_background(8) yields mark_clean_transparent, the full-colour master raster. That single clean master then fans out into every deliverable: vectorize(9) makes the AI/SVG master; select_subject(10)->apply_color_overlay(11) build the single-colour version by laying one flat brand ink over the selected subject, which halftone(12) turns into the print/screen variant; crop_and_resize(13) reframes the same clean master to a wide website/social-header crop (gen-free crop of a wide horizontal slice — no canvas expansion, no compositing); font_recommend(14) sources the style-note typography. No step uses a raw input except the two that legitimately should (HSL reads the palette swatch as colour reference; the single-ink overlay colour is taken from the swatch's locked hex). Every other step strictly consumes a prior step's output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 14 connector calls, 14 DISTINCT Adobe tools, spanning three tool groups (asset_* upload, the image_* raster/tonal/colour/mask/vector/effect family, and font_recommend) with a genuine output->input dependency graph that fans a single cleaned master into five distinct contracted deliverables. It exercises ordering judgment (straighten/crop/tone BEFORE colour push BEFORE background removal; vectorize only AFTER the mark is clean; single-colour version via select_subject->apply_color_overlay before halftone; halftone only on the single-colour output). Every step is headless-confirmed [C] — no [W]/[A]/[T]/[X] capabilities are used: there is NO generative step at all. There is no from-scratch art, no gen-fill, no outpaint/expand, no generative canvas extension, no solid-area-fill step, no compositing onto a new/larger canvas, no layout composition, no Canva, and no Express widget track. The wide social-header deliverable is an honest gen-free reframe via image_crop_and_resize (crop to a wide horizontal slice of the existing master; edges are cropped to hit the ratio, the canvas is not extended). Difficulty also comes from correctly REFUSING the brief's literal from-scratch 'concept sketch' step (not available) and re-scoping to the production-and-finishing work the connector can actually deliver while still hitting every line item of the contracted hand-off (full-colour, single-colour, vector source, high-res PNG/JPEG raster exports, style note with hex/RGB + typography).

reverify.banned_capability_check: NO banned/generative capability remains, and every step is a real, headless [C] Adobe-connector action. No solid-area-fill step and no generative canvas-extension step appear anywhere in the workflow. The single-colour version (steps 10-11) is built by image_select_subject -> image_apply_color_overlay, which lays one flat brand ink over exactly the selected mark subject on transparency — a flat recolour, no generation. The wide social-header deliverable (step 13) is image_crop_and_resize, which REFRAMES the square full-colour master to a wide header aspect by CROPPING to a wide horizontal slice (gen-free; the canvas is not extended/padded, edges are cropped to hit the ratio, and the output is not larger than the source in the widened dimension). No text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no generative outpaint/expand, no upscale/super-resolution, no gen-fill, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no compositing onto a new/larger canvas. image_vectorize (raster->SVG), image_remove_background (mask), image_select_subject, image_apply_color_overlay (flat recolour of the selected subject), image_apply_halftone, image_crop_and_resize, and font_recommend are all non-generative and headless. The whole chain is [C].
reverify.notes: Findings resolved: (1) category set to 'vectorize_logo' (a logo/brand-identity category matching the actual vectorize-and-ship job) instead of the mismatched 'headshot_portrait'; grounding_note prose reconciled to match the field. (2) difficulty_rationale corrected: it now reads 14 connector calls / 14 DISTINCT Adobe tools (matching tool_call_count, distinct_adobe_tools and tools_used) and no longer references a solid-area-fill tool, a mask->invert->solid-fill step, or steps that do not exist. (3) banned_capability_check corrected: no solid-area-fill and no generative canvas-extension step appear anywhere in the workflow; the single-colour version uses image_apply_color_overlay and the wide header uses image_crop_and_resize (gen-free crop, no canvas extension). (4) JPEG completeness gap closed: logo_fullcolour_master.jpg and logo_social_header_crop.jpg added to outputs[] to enumerate the high-res JPEG exports the brief promises alongside the PNGs. Chaining holds: every post-first step consumes a prior output as primary input; the palette swatch is read only where it legitimately grounds colour.
